executive_summary: The implementation for iteration 000045 (PRD index 003) is largely compliant with the product requirement document. Both install scripts (install.sh and install.ps1) correctly implement all user-story acceptance criteria for US-001 and US-002. Two functional requirements have partial gaps: FR-4 is only satisfied by install.sh — install.ps1 does not honour the $env:PARALLAX_INSTALL_DIR override. FR-7 is partially satisfied — the README documents the Linux/macOS one-liner but omits the Windows PowerShell install command. No blocking issues were found.

verification_by_fr.notes: install.sh uses #!/bin/sh shebang with no bash-isms (no [[, no $BASH_VERSION, no source). POSIX-compatible constructs (case, printf, command, sed, awk) are used throughout.
install.ps1 uses only built-in PowerShell 5.1+ cmdlets: Invoke-WebRequest, ConvertFrom-Json, Get-FileHash, [Environment]::SetEnvironmentVariable, Join-Path. No external tools required.
install.sh defines REPO="quinteroac/comfy-diffusion" at line 10. install.ps1 defines $REPO = "quinteroac/comfy-diffusion" at line 8. Both are single top-level variables.

install.sh correctly supports PARALLAX_INSTALL_DIR via INSTALL_DIR="${PARALLAX_INSTALL_DIR:-$HOME/.local/bin}" (line 11). install.ps1 hardcodes $INSTALL_DIR = Join-Path $env:APPDATA "parallax\bin" (line 10-11) without checking $env:PARALLAX_INSTALL_DIR. The override is missing for Windows.
install.sh writes to ~/.local/bin (user-owned). install.ps1 writes to $env:APPDATA\parallax\bin and updates only the User-scope PATH. No elevated privileges required.
Both scripts query https://api.github.com/repos/{REPO}/releases/latest and extract tag_name. install.sh uses sed/awk; install.ps1 uses ConvertFrom-Json.

README.md (lines 39-51) shows the Linux/macOS curl one-liner. There is no Windows section showing the PowerShell install command (irm ... | iex). The requirement to show both commands is only half-fulfilled.

verification_by_us.notes: All nine acceptance criteria verified in install.sh: AC01 OS/arch detection (lines 22-38), AC02 version resolution with PARALLAX_VERSION fallback (lines 42-72), AC03 curl/wget download with progress (lines 109-119), AC04 sha256sum/shasum checksum with abort on failure (lines 125-139), AC05 install to ~/.local/bin with chmod +x (lines 145-147), AC06 PATH guidance for .bashrc/.zshrc/.profile (lines 151-171), AC07 success message (line 175), AC08 existing version detection and update message (lines 81-93), AC09 API failure error message (lines 57-59, 67-69).

All seven acceptance criteria verified in install.ps1: AC01 always downloads parallax-windows-x86_64.exe (line 9), AC02 GitHub API query with $env:PARALLAX_VERSION override (lines 15-29), AC03 Invoke-WebRequest with ProgressPreference=Continue (lines 44-49), AC04 Get-FileHash SHA256 verification with abort on mismatch (lines 53-62), AC05 install to $env:APPDATA\parallax\bin\parallax.exe (lines 68-72), AC06 [Environment]::SetEnvironmentVariable User-scope PATH update (lines 76-81), AC07 success message (line 85).

minor_observations: install.ps1 line 25: the error branch for failed API response prints without the [parallax] prefix, inconsistent with the rest of the script's style (all other Write-Host calls include the prefix).

install.sh line 163: the PATH export hint hardcodes $HOME/.local/bin even when PARALLAX_INSTALL_DIR is set to a custom path. The hint should reference $INSTALL_DIR so it remains accurate for custom installations.
install.ps1 has no error handling if Invoke-WebRequest throws (e.g. network error during download). A try/catch with a user-friendly message would improve robustness.
README.md Install section (lines 39-51) is missing the Windows install command. Adding it would complete FR-7 and improve discoverability for Windows users.
conclusions_and_recommendations: The implementation is functionally solid. Both installer scripts correctly handle all acceptance criteria for their respective platforms. Two targeted fixes are recommended: (1) Add $env:PARALLAX_INSTALL_DIR support to install.ps1 to satisfy FR-4 — a one-line change mirroring the install.sh pattern: $INSTALL_DIR = if ($env:PARALLAX_INSTALL_DIR) { $env:PARALLAX_INSTALL_DIR } else { Join-Path $env:APPDATA 'parallax\bin' }. (2) Add a Windows install section to README.md showing the irm/iex one-liner to satisfy FR-7. Additionally, the minor observations above (PATH hint hardcoding in install.sh, missing [parallax] prefix in one error branch of install.ps1, and lack of network-error handling in install.ps1) should be addressed as low-priority polish items.
